transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective included

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: BusinessNetflix Will Acquire Warner Bros
SOURCE: Forbes

ARTICLE:

BreakingBusinessNetflix Will Acquire Warner Bros. In $83 Billion Deal—Discovery Will Be Split OffBySiladitya Ray,Forbes Staff. Siladitya Ray is a New Delhi-based Forbes news team reporter.Follow AuthorDec 05, 2025, 07:52am ESTDec 05, 2025, 08:53am ESTShareSaveCommentToplineNetflix on Friday announced it had reached a deal to acquire Warner Bros.— including the company's film and television studios and streamer HBO Max—for $82.7 billion, in move that follows a weeks-long bidding war in which the streaming giant beat out Paramount and Comcast.Warner Bros. Discovery has reportedly entered exclusive talks with Netflix for a sale of its studio and TV businesses.Anadolu via Getty ImagesKey FactsIn a press statement the company said the cash and stock deal is valued at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery share and it will close after the media giant's TV network business is spun off into a separate public company, Discovery Global.The deal is subject to regulatory approvals and is expected to be completed in the third quarter of 2026.If it goes through, each WBD shareholder will receive $23.25 in cash per share and $4.501 worth of shares of Netflix's stock.According to Deadline and CNN, Paramount Skydance had offered $27 per share for the entire Warner Bros. Discovery business, including its cable channels like CNN and TNT. Comcast also made a bid only for Warner Bros. Discovery's studio and streaming businesses.Bloomberg previously reported that Netflix is offering Warner Bros. Discovery a $5 billion breakup fee if regulators don't clear the deal.
---

```json
{
  "summary": "Netflix has announced an agreement to acquire Warner Bros., including its film and television studios and the HBO Max streaming service, for $82.7 billion. The deal, valued at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery share, will proceed after the company's TV network business is spun off into a separate public entity called Discovery Global. Netflix emerged victorious from a competitive bidding process that included rival bids from Paramount Skydance and Comcast. Under the terms, Warner Bros. Discovery shareholders will receive $23.25 in cash and $4.50 in Netflix stock per share. The transaction requires regulatory approval and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026. Netflix has reportedly offered a $5 billion breakup fee if regulators block the deal. Paramount Skydance had offered $27 per share for the entire Warner Bros. Discovery business including cable channels, while Comcast bid only for the studio and streaming assets.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Netflix will acquire Warner Bros. for $82.7 billion in a cash and stock deal valued at $27.75 per share",
    "The acquisition includes Warner Bros.' film and television studios and the HBO Max streaming platform",
    "Warner Bros. Discovery's TV network business will be spun off into a separate public company called Discovery Global before the deal closes",
    "Netflix beat competing bids from Paramount Skydance ($27 per share for entire business) and Comcast (studios and streaming only)",
    "The transaction requires regulatory approval and is expected to close in Q3 2026, with a $5 billion breakup fee if blocked"
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "Forbes",
    "Deadline",
    "CNN",
    "Bloomberg"
  ]
}
```

parsed_output.summary: Netflix has announced an agreement to acquire Warner Bros., including its film and television studios and the HBO Max streaming service, for $82.7 billion. The deal, valued at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery share, will proceed after the company's TV network business is spun off into a separate public entity called Discovery Global. Netflix emerged victorious from a competitive bidding process that included rival bids from Paramount Skydance and Comcast. Under the terms, Warner Bros. Discovery shareholders will receive $23.25 in cash and $4.50 in Netflix stock per share. The transaction requires regulatory approval and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026. Netflix has reportedly offered a $5 billion breakup fee if regulators block the deal. Paramount Skydance had offered $27 per share for the entire Warner Bros. Discovery business including cable channels, while Comcast bid only for the studio and streaming assets.
key_facts: Netflix will acquire Warner Bros. for $82.7 billion in a cash and stock deal valued at $27.75 per share
The acquisition includes Warner Bros.' film and television studios and the HBO Max streaming platform
Warner Bros. Discovery's TV network business will be spun off into a separate public company called Discovery Global before the deal closes
Netflix beat competing bids from Paramount Skydance ($27 per share for entire business) and Comcast (studios and streaming only)
The transaction requires regulatory approval and is expected to close in Q3 2026, with a $5 billion breakup fee if blocked
